claude_reasoning.reasoning: Capital expenditures are modeled as positive values consistently across all periods. This is a valid modeling convention - many financial models use positive values for capex and apply the sign in calculations.
Material costs are consistently positive across all periods. This appears to be a modeling convention where costs are represented as positive values.
Operating expenses are consistently positive across all periods. This is a standard modeling approach where expense values are positive and signs are handled in calculations.
Debt service values are consistently positive across operational periods (2026-2035) and show decreasing trend typical of amortizing debt. This is a valid modeling convention.
Same pattern as flag 0 - consistent positive capex modeling convention.
Same pattern as flag 1 - consistent positive material costs modeling convention.
Same pattern as flag 2 - consistent positive operating expenses modeling convention.
Depreciation is consistently positive (880.5) during operations. This is a valid approach where depreciation values are positive and applied as deductions in income statement calculations.
Interest expense values are positive and show decreasing trend (494.49 to 54.94) consistent with declining debt balance. This is a standard modeling convention.
Tax expenses are positive and show reasonable values relative to earnings before tax (~30% effective rate). This is standard modeling practice.
Duplicate of flag 3 - debt service consistently modeled as positive values.
Same debt service pattern continues - decreasing positive values consistent with amortizing debt schedule.
